summary: Backend regression suite (39 tests) for KRINOS executed against public URL. 29 PASS / 10 FAIL. Auth (login/me/JWT bearer), seed data (12 propuestas, 6 jurados, 3 ternas, 24 evaluaciones, 11 campos, 4 catálogos, 9 criterios, 7 desempates), reads on all major endpoints, RBAC (jurado→/users 403, /auditoria 403), reports/dashboard/excel, evaluación individual full flow (Borrador→En edición→Finalizada→Firmada + acta PDF), and asignación masiva-subregion all PASS. CRITICAL: every POST that returns the inserted doc fails with 500 due to MongoDB `_id` ObjectId leaking into FastAPI response (jsonable_encoder ValueError). This blocks create-propuesta, create-jurado, create-terna, create-asignacion, create-catalogo, create-campo, create-criterio, evaluaciones-colectivas POST, and ranking generation (rankings.insert_one then returns doc).

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error - Motor insert_one mutates the dict adding ObjectId `_id`; the route then returns the same dict and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder raises ValueError [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable")]. Fix: after every `await db.X.insert_one(doc)` add `doc.pop('_id', None)` (or build a new dict for the response). Backend log confirms /root/.venv/.../fastapi/encoders.py serialization error.

Lockout did not trigger after 5 failed attempts to a fixed username; 6th attempt returned 401 instead of 429. Possible cause: ingress propagates a different client.host per request (k8s pod IP varies) so lock_key f"{ip}:{ident}" is never reused. Recommend keying lockout on identifier only, or on x-forwarded-for. The brute-force protection is effectively bypassed in production.

minor.issue: EmailStr (pydantic email-validator) rejects RFC reserved TLDs like .test and .example with 422. Acceptable behavior, but documentation/test data should use real-looking domains (e.g. @krinos.gov.co).

Spec said '24 individuales + 12 colectivas'. Actual seed creates colectivas/individuales only for the 8 propuestas whose subregion is mapped to a terna (Urabá/Oriente/Norte). Total = 8 colectivas + 24 individuales = 32. The 4 propuestas in Bajo Cauca/Suroeste/Magdalena Medio have no terna assigned. Either expand subregion_to_terna mapping or update spec.

action_items: FIX (critical): strip Mongo `_id` after every insert_one before returning the doc. Affected files: /app/backend/routes_config.py (create_convocatoria, create_catalogo, create_campo, create_criterio, create_desempate), /app/backend/routes_data.py (create_propuesta, create_jurado, create_terna, create_asignacion, masiva-subregion - but masiva doesn't return doc so OK), /app/backend/routes_eval.py (crear_eval_colectiva), /app/backend/routes_reports.py (generar_ranking - it does `await db.rankings.insert_one(resultado); return resultado`). Quickest patch is `doc.pop('_id', None)` after insert, OR insert a copy: `await db.X.insert_one({**doc})`.
FIX (security): brute-force lockout currently bypassable in k8s ingress. Use identifier-only or X-Forwarded-For-derived IP as lock key.
Consider expanding subregion→terna mapping in seed_demo_data so every propuesta has assignations (or document the limitation).
critical_code_review_comments: MongoDB ObjectId leakage: pattern `doc['id']=...; await coll.insert_one(doc); return doc` is used in 9+ POST handlers and is broken. Motor's insert_one mutates the input dict adding `_id` (ObjectId). FastAPI jsonable_encoder cannot serialize it.
Brute-force lock key includes client IP which is unstable behind k8s ingress; effective protection is missing.

auth.py uses `bcrypt` + `$2b$` hash format (correct), but `secure=False` cookies on set_auth_cookies — fine because the spec uses Bearer tokens, but cookies are still being set on every login and never used by tests.
GET endpoints already strip `_id` with projection `{'_id':0}` - apply the same discipline to POST returns.
Email validator rejects .test/.example TLDs — be explicit in API docs.
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_-prefixed propuestas/criterios/campos/catalogos/users (those that succeeded). Most POST creates failed due to the ObjectId bug.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the `_id` leak, re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Expect the 8 currently-failing POST/colectiva/ranking tests to pass. The brute-force test may still fail depending on ingress IP handling. The asignaciones_seed expectation can be loosened to >=32 OR seed mapping expanded.
rca of the issue: Motor's AsyncIOMotorCollection.insert_one(document) mutates the passed dict, setting `document['_id']` to a bson.ObjectId. Routes that do `await db.X.insert_one(doc)` then `return doc` end up returning that dict including `_id`. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot encode bson.ObjectId → ValueError [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable")] → 500. Reproduction: POST /api/propuestas with valid admin token and minimal payload → 500. Mitigation: insert a copy (`await db.X.insert_one({**doc})`) or pop `_id` after insert (`doc.pop('_id', None)`).
